title: sorbus sambucifolia
intro: Sorbus sambucifolia, also known as the Elderflower Ash or Mountain Ash, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to mountainous regions of Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins. The plant is said to offer various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and providing antioxidant properties. In herbal preparations, Sorbus sambucifolia can be consumed as a tea or used in infusions, tinctures, and infusions to harness its medicinal properties.
